chunks.content: The authentication middleware validates the bearer token before route handlers run. If the token is missing or expired, the request stops immediately with a 401 response.
Tests verify that expired tokens never reach the protected route handler and that the API returns 401 consistently.
Authentication runs before business logic so the pipeline fails fast, reduces wasted work, and keeps authorization checks consistent across all routes.
Maybe we should later revisit CSS modules and maybe there was also something about auth but the thread drifted and repeated the same concerns several times.
INFO start INFO start INFO start debug trace debug trace debug trace request heartbeat request heartbeat request heartbeat.
Keep validation close to the boundary. The earlier the server rejects invalid auth state, the easier it is to reason about downstream handlers.
